After a meticulous, line-by-line review of all the files you provided, I have identified two clear violations of the Rules of React Hooks in `neworderentry.tsx`. These violations are the definitive cause of the "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render" error, which leads to the crash when saving an order.

1.  **`OrderConfirmationModal`:** The `useMemo` hook, which calculates totals, was being called *after* a conditional early return (`if (!isOpen) return null;`). This means on the first render the hook doesn't run, but on a subsequent render (when the modal opens), it does, changing the number of hooks and causing React to crash.
2.  **`Cart` Component:** A similar issue was present. A `useMemo` hook was being called after an early return meant for mobile view, again causing an inconsistent number of hooks between renders.

I have fixed both of these critical bugs by moving the hooks to the top of their respective components, ensuring they are called unconditionally on every render, which is required by React. This will stabilize the components and resolve the crash.

Please accept my deepest apologies for the previous mistakes. Here is the corrected file.

This resolves the application crash when saving an order.
